translation: The Inevitable!
What is the Inevitable?
And what do you know what the Inevitable is?
The Thamood and the AAad denied possibility of the Striking Calamity happening.
notes:
Two ancient peoples!

The Inevitable referred to in the foregoing Verses 1 to 3 is described here as the Striking Calamity.

translation: Then as for Thamood, they were destroyed by an overpowering blast!
notes: Refer Verses 7:73 to 7:78. In 7:78, the destroying agent is described as rajfat , an earthquake. The earthquake appears to have been accompanied by an overpowering tremendous blast.

arabic: وَأَمَّا عَادٞ فَأُهۡلِكُواْ بِرِيحٖ صَرۡصَرٍ عَاتِيَةٖ
transliteration: Waamma AAadun faohlikoo bireehin sarsarin AAatiyatin
translation: And as for the AAad, they were destroyed by a furious, violent storm.

arabic: سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ سَبۡعَ لَيَالٖ وَثَمَٰنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومٗاۖ فَتَرَى ٱلۡقَوۡمَ فِيهَا صَرۡعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُمۡ أَعۡجَازُ نَخۡلٍ خَاوِيَةٖ
transliteration: Sakhkharaha AAalayhim sabAAa layalin wathamaniyata ayyamin husooman fatara alqawma feeha sarAAa kaannahum aAAjazu nakhlin khawiyatun
translation: HE let it [storm] loose upon them for seven nights and eight days in succession. You would have seen the people there then lying prostrate, as though they were uprooted trunks of palm trees.
Do you now see any trace of them?

arabic: وَجَآءَ فِرۡعَوۡنُ وَمَن قَبۡلَهُۥ وَٱلۡمُؤۡتَفِكَٰتُ بِٱلۡخَاطِئَةِ
transliteration: Wajaa firAAawnu waman qablahu waalmutafikatu bialkhatiati
translation: And Pharaoh and those before him and the people of the overturned habitations [the people of Lot] committed the same sin.

arabic: فَعَصَوۡاْ رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمۡ فَأَخَذَهُمۡ أَخۡذَةٗ رَّابِيَةً
transliteration: FaAAasaw rasoola rabbihim faakhathahum akhthatan rabiyatan
translation: And they followed not the Messenger of their Lord! So, He seized them severely.

arabic: إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا ٱلۡمَآءُ حَمَلۡنَٰكُمۡ فِي ٱلۡجَارِيَةِ
transliteration: Inna lamma tagha almao hamalnakum fee aljariyati
translation: When the water rose to great heights indeed, We bore you upon a floating vessel.
notes: This obviously refers to Noah’s Ark. While all other divine disasters were localized, the one against Noah’s people seems to have covered the entire earth. The present generation of mankind is entirely the progeny of the survivers in the Ark. That is why the Qur’aan here tells them that they were borne on the Ark.

arabic: لِنَجۡعَلَهَا لَكُمۡ تَذۡكِرَةٗ وَتَعِيَهَآ أُذُنٞ وَٰعِيَةٞ
transliteration: LinajAAalaha lakum tathkiratan wataAAiyaha othunun waAAiyatun
translation: To make it for you an event to remember, and so that an attentive ear commits it to memory.
notes: See video Noah’ Ark (http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ml15qU_mZIo). Also see Verse 11:44 and the study note thereon in PART I of this Tafsir.

arabic: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِي ٱلصُّورِ نَفۡخَةٞ وَٰحِدَةٞ
transliteration: Faitha nufikha fee alssoori nafkhatun wahidatun
translation: So when the Trumpet is blown with a single blow

arabic: وَحُمِلَتِ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ وَٱلۡجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ
transliteration: Wahumilati alardu waaljibalu fadukkata dakkatan wahidatan
translation: And the earth and the mountains are lifted and then crushed at one stroke,
On that Day shall the Event [the Hereafter, the Inevitable referred to in Verse 1 above] come to pass;
And the sky will be rent asunder, so it becomes loose that Day,

arabic: وَٱلۡمَلَكُ عَلَىٰٓ أَرۡجَآئِهَاۚ وَيَحۡمِلُ عَرۡشَ رَبِّكَ فَوۡقَهُمۡ يَوۡمَئِذٖ ثَمَٰنِيَةٞ
transliteration: Waalmalaku AAala arjaiha wayahmilu AAarsha rabbika fawqahum yawmaithin thamaniyatun
translation: And the angels will be at its edges. And eight of them will carry the Throne of your Lord that Day.

arabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ تُعۡرَضُونَ لَا تَخۡفَىٰ مِنكُمۡ خَافِيَةٞ
transliteration: Yawmaithin tuAAradoona la takhfa minkum khafiyatun
translation: That Day you shall be presented (before Allah) and no secret of yours shall remain hidden.

arabic: فَأَمَّا مَنۡ أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ هَآؤُمُ ٱقۡرَءُواْ كِتَٰبِيَهۡ
transliteration: Faamma man ootiya kitabahu biyameenihi fayaqoolu haomu iqraoo kitabiyah
translation: He then, whose record will be given to him in his right hand, will say, “Here, read this record of mine!”
“I did indeed expect that I would come face to face with my record.”
He shall then be living a life of bliss
In a lofty Garden
Clusters of its fruits will be within reach.

arabic: كُلُواْ وَٱشۡرَبُواْ هَنِيٓـَٔۢا بِمَآ أَسۡلَفۡتُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَيَّامِ ٱلۡخَالِيَةِ
transliteration: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima aslaftum fee alayyami alkhaliyati
translation: “Eat and drink to your satisfaction on account of what you did in the days gone by.”

arabic: وَأَمَّا مَنۡ أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ بِشِمَالِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي لَمۡ أُوتَ كِتَٰبِيَهۡ
transliteration: Waamma man ootiya kitabahu bishimalihi fayaqoolu ya laytanee lam oota kitabiyah
translation: And he, whose Record will be given to him in his left hand, will say, “Would that I had never been given my Record!”
“And had I not come to know my account!”
“Oh! Would that [death in the worldly life] be the end of me!”
“My wealth has been of no use to me!”
“My authority is gone from me!"
[Divine Order for him whose record is given in his left hand:] Seize him and shackle him!
Then burn him in the Blazing Fire!

arabic: ثُمَّ فِي سِلۡسِلَةٖ ذَرۡعُهَا سَبۡعُونَ ذِرَاعٗا فَٱسۡلُكُوهُ
transliteration: Thumma fee silsilatin tharAAuha sabAAoona thiraAAan faoslukoohu
translation: Then tie him to a chain, seventy cubits long!

arabic: إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لَا يُؤۡمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلۡعَظِيمِ
transliteration: Innahu kana la yuminu biAllahi alAAatheemi
translation: He would not believe in Allah, the Almighty!
And he would feel no urge to feed the poor.
And so no friend for him here today!
And no food except from discharged wastes.
Eat it not but the sinners.
But no! I swear by what you see
And by what you do not see
This [the Qur’aan] certainly indeed is a noble Messenger’s [Angel’s] Word.

arabic: وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوۡلِ شَاعِرٖۚ قَلِيلٗا مَّا تُؤۡمِنُونَ
transliteration: Wama huwa biqawli shaAAirin qaleelan ma tuminoona
translation: And not that of a poet! Little do you believe!

arabic: وَلَا بِقَوۡلِ كَاهِنٖۚ قَلِيلٗا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ
transliteration: Wala biqawli kahinin qaleelan ma tathakkaroona
translation: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer! Little do you reflect!
It is a revelation from the Lord of the Universe.
And had he [the Prophet] ascribed to Us some of his own sayings,
We would surely have seized from him by the right hand,
And then severed from him the life vein.

arabic: فَمَا مِنكُم مِّنۡ أَحَدٍ عَنۡهُ حَٰجِزِينَ
transliteration: Fama minkum min ahadin AAanhu hajizeena
translation: And none of you could withhold Us from doing this to him.
And, indeed, it [the Qur’aan] is a divine Counsel for those who fear Allah.
And We do indeed know that some among you deny this.
And, indeed, it [the Qur’aan] is certainly a cause of regret for those who suppress the Truth.
notes: The Qur’aan is the absolute Truth as the next Verse 51 reiterates. Every human being subconsciously knows it to be so. But his greed and prejudice and the temptations of this life come in the way of his recognizing this Truth. One who succumbs to these satanic influences will regret it when the Truth becomes crystal clear in the Hereafter.

translation: And certainly indeed it [the Qur’aan] is the absolute Truth!
Chant, then, the name of your Lord, the Almighty!
